summary: Iontophoresis typically uses strong but safe fluorides due to its requirement for high efficacy; while specific percentages vary based on context, common practice often favors around 2%. Agent1 concludes by emphasizing the importance of using appropriate concentration levels tailored towards achieving both therapeutic benefits and patient safety during treatments involving iontophoresis. This summary captures two main aspects crucially related to understanding how fluoride dosage works within an iontophoresis procedure—its strength relative to desired outcomes versus necessary safeguards—and highlights why options like 2%, which balances power and caution appropriately, might be preferred clinically despite not being explicitly stated across all sources consulted directly
